For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MEN CHEW GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/2/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed. (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) Item 8 of the petition indicates that the four residuary beneficiaries are a niece and nephews, but if they are the children of the decedent's cousin, they are first cousins once removed. A supplement to the third amended petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to clarify the relationships to the decedent of the persons identified on Attachment 8. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/18/25. After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required, as the will was not self-proving. None of the witnesses signed under penalty of perjury, and the jurat was not completed; the notary only acknowledged the signatures. No Proof of Subscribing Witness has been filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEFFERY D. PEARL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 12/19/17. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. (NB: The pleading form of proof of service filed on 4/23/25 did not have attached to it a NEW Notice of Petition to Administer the Estate showing the 5/19/25 hearing date; a copy of the POS that was filed on 12/18/24, showing the 1/27/25 hearing, was attached. Since the pleading form of Proof of Service showed "Date: May 19, 2025" on the cover sheet, the Court waives any defect in the confusing notice.) Status hearing will be set for 6/1/26. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 6/1/26, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IN RE: BIANCE PUTTERS REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order Determining Interest In Community Property And Directing Trustee To Transfer Community Property Portion To The Petitioner
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/30/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) Notice is insufficient. Rosa de Anda, UCLA Cancer Center Foundation, and Lungevity Foundation are beneficiaries of the trust and were not given notice of the petition (Probate Code Section 17203(a)(2)). 2) The trust instrument was not attached to the amended petition, which supercedes the initial filing, which does have the trust instrument attached. 3) A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person (CRC 7.51(a)(2)). 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court. Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition. (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than June 16, 2025, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANET LYNN MACRITCHIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/2/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption is incomplete if the petitioner is seeking IAEA authority. (2) A written declination must be filed by or on behalf of an individual who has higher priority than the petitioner for issuance of letters of administration but does not desire to act. [LRSF 14.24.] (3) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (5) The petitioner, the person named in item 2 of the petition, is missing from item 8 of the petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/18/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 7b(3) of the petition does not need to be completed when 7b is not checked. There is no need to attach a copy of a petition to the notice of the hearing of that petition, but the instructions for how to appear remotely must be attached to any notice.)
